expectations.text: Output includes a Verdict line (REQUEST CHANGES | COMMENT ONLY | NEEDS SCOPE | NO MAJOR FINDINGS)
expectations.evidence: Opens with '## Verdict' followed by 'REQUEST CHANGES'.
Findings are headed '### 1.', '### 2.', ... '### 5.' Plain numeric headings; no CQ-001-style stable IDs anywhere in the output.

expectations.text: Each finding includes Location, Evidence, Why it matters, Recommended remediation, and Confidence
expectations.evidence: Each numbered finding includes the five fields, e.g. Finding 1 has 'Location:', 'Evidence:', 'Why it matters:' (twice), 'Recommended remediation:', 'Confidence: High'.

expectations.text: Identifies the non-atomic payment/order update where charge can succeed while the order is never marked paid
expectations.evidence: Finding 1 [Blocker] 'Payment is charged before the order is validated' — 'a charge has already been attempted, and the order row is never updated to paid. This leaves the system in a half-applied state (money moved, order not marked paid).'

expectations.text: Identifies the `any` type-boundary and silent empty-object fallback
expectations.evidence: Finding 3 'Pervasive any typing hides the real contracts at the boundary'; Finding 5 covers the call site where '`?? {}` fallback is applied' and recommends a typed return instead of 'masking it with ?? {}'.
Static read-only review; no claim of modification. Scope limitations: 'the eval workspace contains only order_service.ts' and remediations are advisory.
